paper_title: Functional analysis of CjTKPR1 using A. thaliana

methods.method_name: CjTKPR1 Complementation Test in A. thaliana
methods.description: Complementation tests were conducted using Arabidopsis thaliana to confirm the function of CjTKPR1 sequences in controlling pollen production. This involved introducing different CjTKPR1 variants into an A. thaliana mutant lacking pollen and assessing the restoration of pollen production.

steps.description: First, we obtained the TKPR1 mutant of A. thaliana (Attkpr1-1 mutant, SAIL_837_D01), which homozygous plant shows a pollen-free phenotype ( Fig. 4B and C) (37).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain *Arabidopsis thaliana* Attkpr1-1 mutant seeds or plants

steps.description: Then, four CjTKPR1 sequences, i.e. the WT, ms4 mutant (MT), WT background with a point mutation at 244 nt (T to C, WM), and ms4 background with a point mutation at 244 nt (C to T, MW) (Fig. 4A), were each introduced into heterozygous plants of AtTKPR1 (AtTKPR1/Attkpr1-1), not into Attkpr1-1 homozygous plants (Attkpr1-1/Attkpr1-1) that produce no pollen and therefore obtain no progenies.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and introduce CjTKPR1 variant constructs into heterozygous *A. thaliana* plants
inputs: Site-directed mutagenesis reagents (for WM, MW variants)
outputs: CjTKPR1 expression constructs (WT, MT, WM, MW)

steps.description: Next, we selected Attkpr1-1 homozygous plants with CjTKPR1 constructs from the T2 or T3 generations.
steps.searchable_summary: Screen T2 or T3 generation plants to select homozygous Attkpr1-1 individuals carrying CjTKPR1 constructs
inputs: Genotyping methods (e.g., PCR for T-DNA insertion and Attkpr1-1 homozygosity)
outputs: Selected homozygous Attkpr1-1 *A. thaliana* plants containing CjTKPR1 constructs

steps.description: Anthers of these plants were stained by Alexander staining (44).
steps.searchable_summary: Stain anthers of selected plants using Alexander staining
inputs: Anthers from selected homozygous Attkpr1-1 plants with constructs

steps.searchable_summary: Observe stained anthers under a microscope to confirm pollen production
outputs: Pollen production phenotype (present/absent) for each plant/construct

steps.description: As a result, CjTKPR1-WT-introduced plants recovered pollen production (Fig. 4D), whereas CjTKPR1-MT-introduced plants still showed the pollen-free phenotype (Fig. 4E). These results indicate that CjTKPR1-WT can complement pollen production in A. thaliana and that the CjTKPR1 allelic difference controls pollen production. Lines with a single-nucleotide substitution at 244 nt showed that CjTKPR1-WM-introduced plants, which have a CjTKPR1-WT background with a point mutation at 244 nt, could not recover pollen production (Fig. 4F). However, CjTKPR1-MW-introduced plants, which have a ms4 background without a point mutation at 244 nt, recovered pollen production (Fig. 4G). Together, these complementation test results indicate that single-nucleotide substitution of CjTKPR1 at 244 nt determines pollen production.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pollen production phenotypes to determine complementation by different CjTKPR1 variants
inputs: Pollen production phenotypes for plants with CjTKPR1-WT, MT, WM, and MW constructs
outputs: Conclusion on the functional complementation ability of each CjTKPR1 variant
Determination of the effect of the 244 nt mutation on CjTKPR1 function
